name: Trevor Project
description: The leading suicide prevention and crisis intervention organization for LGBTQ+ young people under 25. Operates TrevorLifeline, TrevorChat, and TrevorText.

name: Trans Lifeline
description: A peer support and crisis hotline run by and for trans people. Operators are always trans-identified. Also offers a microgrants program for legal name and gender marker changes.

name: Crisis Text Line
description: Free 24/7 text-based crisis support. While not LGBTQ+-specific, it is widely used by LGBTQ+ people and trains counselors on LGBTQ+ issues.

name: GLAAD
description: Originally the Gay and Lesbian Alliance Against Defamation, GLAAD works through entertainment, news, and digital media to shape narratives and change culture for LGBTQ+ acceptance.

name: Human Rights Campaign
description: The largest LGBTQ+ civil rights organization in the US, focused on federal legislation, the Corporate Equality Index, and electing pro-equality candidates.

name: PFLAG
description: The first and largest organization for LGBTQ+ people, their families, and allies. Provides support groups, educational resources, and advocacy. Has chapters in all 50 US states.

name: Lambda Legal
description: The oldest and largest legal organization working for the civil rights of LGBTQ+ people and those living with HIV. Pursues litigation, education, and policy work.

name: National Center for Transgender Equality
description: The leading social justice advocacy organization for transgender people in the US. Produces the National Transgender Discrimination Survey and advocates for federal policy changes.

name: GLSEN
description: Works to create safe and inclusive K-12 schools for LGBTQ+ students. Runs the GSA (Gender and Sexuality Alliance) Network and conducts the National School Climate Survey.

name: It Gets Better Project
description: A non-profit that uplifts, empowers, and connects LGBTQ+ youth through storytelling. Founded in 2010 after Dan Savage and Terry Miller posted a YouTube video in response to a wave of LGBTQ+ youth suicides.

name: ACLU LGBTQ Rights Project
description: The ACLU's dedicated LGBTQ+ rights project pursues litigation and advocacy on a broad range of issues including marriage equality, adoption, employment discrimination, and anti-trans laws.

name: Howard Brown Health
description: One of the nation's largest LGBTQ+-focused healthcare organizations, based in Chicago. Provides comprehensive primary care, HIV services, mental health, and research.

name: Fenway Health
description: LGBTQ+-focused community health center in Boston providing primary care, mental health, HIV/AIDS services, and conducting significant research on LGBTQ+ health disparities.

name: ILGA World
description: The International Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Trans and Intersex Association — a worldwide federation of over 1,700 member organizations. Publishes the annual State-Sponsored Homophobia report tracking criminalization globally.

name: Stonewall UK
description: The largest LGBTQ+ charity in the UK and Europe. Known for its Workplace Equality Index and campaigns for legislative change including same-sex marriage in England and Wales.

name: OutRight Action International
description: Advocates for the human rights of LGBTIQ+ people at the United Nations. Works to advance inclusion and protection within the international human rights framework.

name: Rainbow Health Ontario
description: A provincial program that builds capacity in the Ontario health and social services sector to provide more effective services to LGBTQ+ communities through training, research, and resources.

name: SAGE
description: The world's largest and oldest organization dedicated to improving the lives of LGBTQ+ older adults. Provides direct services, advocacy, and operates the SAGE National LGBT Elder Hotline.

name: National Queer and Trans Therapists of Color Network
description: A healing justice organization committed to transforming mental health for queer and trans people of color. Maintains a directory of QTPOC therapists and organizes around collective healing.

name: Intersex Human Rights Australia
description: An intersex-led organization advocating for the human rights of intersex people in Australia. Created the intersex pride symbol and engages in UN treaty body processes.

name: PFLAG Canada
description: Canada's only national organization that provides support, resources, and education to LGBTQ+ people and their families. Has chapters across Canada.

name: Youth Line
description: A peer-support community organization serving LGBTQ+ youth in Ontario, Canada. Offers phone, text, and online chat support from trained LGBTQ+ peer volunteers.

name: AVEN (Asexual Visibility and Education Network)
description: The largest online community for asexual, aromantic, and questioning people. Provides educational resources and hosts the world's largest asexual community forum.

name: Minus18
description: Australia's largest youth-led organization for LGBTQ+ young people. Provides social events, mental health resources, and school programs to improve wellbeing for LGBTQ+ youth aged under 26.

name: GLBTQ Legal Advocates & Defenders (GLAD)
description: New England's leading LGBTQ+ legal rights organization. Litigated Goodridge v. Department of Public Health (2003), which led to Massachusetts becoming the first US state to legalize same-sex marriage.
